# UC-22.48.3 · Developer-to-production SoD — same developer submits AND approves merge

## Description

Direct SoD control for merge-to-production: author and approver must be distinct users.

## Value

Makes a core deployment-pipeline SoD requirement a continuously-enforced technical control.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure Git provider captures author + approver; (2) run hourly; (3) on fire, revert merge via pipeline automation. Indexes required: `index=git`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=bitbucket:audit`, `sourcetype=github:audit`, `sourcetype=gitlab:audit`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Git provider audit logs (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket), CI/CD deployment events..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure Git provider captures author + approver; (2) run hourly; (3) on fire, revert merge via pipeline automation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=git sourcetype=github:audit OR sourcetype=gitlab:audit OR sourcetype=bitbucket:audit action=merge earliest=-1h
| search target_branch IN (main,master,release*)
| where author=approver
| table _time repo target_branch author approver commit_sha
```

Understanding this SPL

**Developer-to-production SoD — same developer submits AND approves merge** — Makes a core deployment-pipeline SoD requirement a continuously-enforced technical control.

Documented **Data sources**: Git provider audit logs (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket), CI/CD deployment events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: git; **sourcetype**: github:audit, gitlab:audit, bitbucket:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=git, sourcetype=github:audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Filters the current rows with `where author=approver` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, approver, author, commit_sha, repo, target_branch.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of violations, bar chart by repo, single-value 'merges SoD-compliant (30d)'.

## SPL

```spl
index=git sourcetype=github:audit OR sourcetype=gitlab:audit OR sourcetype=bitbucket:audit action=merge earliest=-1h
| search target_branch IN (main,master,release*)
| where author=approver
| table _time repo target_branch author approver commit_sha
```

## Visualization

Table of violations, bar chart by repo, single-value 'merges SoD-compliant (30d)'.
